definitions: Too Long; Didn't Read. Used to indicate that a preceding text was excessively long and the speaker/writer did not read it, or to introduce a summary of a longer text.
A summary of a longer text, often placed at the beginning or end of the original content.
examples: TL;DR: I'm not reading all that.
Here's the TL;DR of the article.
The report was so long, my TL;DR was just 'it's complicated'.
